basic_details.job_title: CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 100 Assistant Section Officer Posts
basic_details.post_name: Assistant Section Officer (ASO) - General / Finance & Accounts / Stores & Purchase
basic_details.short_information: Daily Current Affairs 23 June 2026: Download Free PDF Now

application_fee.details: The application fee for CSIR ASO 2026 is payable online through SBI Collect. Women candidates, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates are fully exempted from paying any fee. Fee-paying candidates must upload the e-Receipt in the online application before the submission deadline.

qualification.details: The eligibility for CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026 is highly specific and differs from standard government job notifications. Only candidates who appeared in UPSC's Civil Services Examination 2024 but were not finally recommended for any service - and whose details appear on the UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal - are eligible. General graduates not associated with CSE-2024 are not eligible for this recruitment. | Candidates must hold a University degree and must be non-recommended candidates of UPSC Civil Services Examination 2024 (CSE-2024), as per details available on the UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal. There is no branch or subject restriction on the degree - any graduate from a recognised university qualifies on academic grounds, provided the CSE-2024 condition is met. | Candidates wondering about the age limit for CSIR ASO 2026 should note that the upper age limit is 33 years as on the closing date of online application. Age relaxation is available for reserved categories as listed below.

vacancy_details.Disability Type: Locomotor disability (CP, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy)
Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability & Mental Illness

pwd_wise_breakdown.disability_type: Locomotor disability (CP, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy)
Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability & Mental Illness

salary_details.da: As applicable at Central Government rates
salary_details.hra: As applicable (not admissible if CSIR accommodation availed)
salary_details.details: Candidates selected as Assistant Section Officer in CSIR will be placed at Pay Level 7, Cell 1 of the 7th Pay Commission Pay Matrix, with a starting basic pay of ₹44,900 per month scaling up to ₹1,42,400. Beyond the base salary, CSIR employees receive a comprehensive benefits package aligned with Central Government norms. | Officers appointed will be liable to serve anywhere in India across any of CSIR's 37 national laboratories, institutes, extension centres, or CSIR Headquarters at Anusandhan Bhawan, New Delhi. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: The CSIR ASO 2026 selection process involves two key stages - shortlisting based on CSE-2024 marks
followed by a Computer Proficiency Test (CPT). The final merit is determined by CSE-2024 total marks (Mains + Personality Test)
subject to qualifying the CPT. Candidates who clear the CPT but score lower in CSE-2024 will rank below those with higher CSE-2024 scores
making UPSC marks the decisive factor.
Application Screening: Applications are screened and candidates are shortlisted for CPT in the ratio of 1:5 (i.e.

subject to a minimum of five). Shortlisting is based on total marks obtained in UPSC CSE-2024 Mains plus Personality Test. Computer Proficiency Test (CPT): Shortlisted candidates appear for a qualifying CPT of 100 marks and 1-hour duration. Qualifying the CPT is mandatory for further consideration. Final Merit List: Prepared based on total CSE-2024 marks (Mains + Personality Test)

subject to qualifying the CPT with the minimum threshold marks prescribed by CSIR. Cadre Allocation: A single merit list is drawn. CSIR allocates cadre (General / Finance & Accounts / Stores & Purchase) based on merit and the candidate's choice submitted in the online application. Cadre once allotted is final and unchangeable. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates are called to produce original documents for verification.
In case of a tie in scores
CSIR resolves it in the following order of precedence:
Higher marks in Mains Examination of CSE-2024 Date of Birth - older candidate placed higher Earlier acquisition of Essential Degree Alphabetical order of first names

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in CSIR Assistant Section Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: CSIR has announced 100 vacancies for the post of Assistant Section Officer under Advertisement No. E-I/RC/2026/2. These include 61 posts for ASO (General), 18 for ASO (Finance & Accounts), and 21 for ASO (Stores & Purchase). Additionally, 7 posts out of the 100 are reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible to apply for CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only candidates who are non-recommended candidates of UPSC Civil Services Examination 2024 (CSE-2024) and hold a University degree are eligible. Candidates must appear on the UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal. General graduates who did not appear in CSE-2024 are not eligible for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for CSIR ASO 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for both online application submission and fee payment is 13 July 2026 at 05:00 PM. Applications must be submitted through https://www.csir.res.in or https://recruitment.csir.res.in.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for CSIR Assistant Section Officer?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be appointed at Pay Level 7, Cell 1 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix, with a basic pay range of ₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400 per month, along with DA, HRA, Transport Allowance, and other Central Government service benefits.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for CSIR ASO 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates from UR, OBC (NCL), and EWS categories must pay ₹500 via SBI Collect. Women, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates are exempted from paying any application fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection involves three stages: (1) shortlisting based on CSE-2024 Mains + Personality Test marks in a 1:5 ratio per category; (2) a qualifying Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) of 100 marks and 1-hour duration; and (3) final merit prepared on the basis of CSE-2024 total marks, subject to clearing CPT minimum threshold marks.

faq.question: Q7. What is the Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) for CSIR ASO?
faq.answer: The CPT is a 100-mark, 1-hour qualifying test covering Word Processing, Spreadsheet preparation, and PowerPoint Presentation. Minimum qualifying marks range from 30 (PwBD categories) to 40 (UR category) out of 100. The CPT is tentatively scheduled to be held in Delhi.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for CSIR ASO 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 33 years as on 13 July 2026 (the closing date). Age relaxation is available: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), 10 years for PwBD (UR), 15 years for PwBD (SC/ST), 13 years for PwBD (OBC), and 5 years for CSIR departmental candidates.

faq.question: Q9. How will cadre be allotted - General, Finance & Accounts, or Stores & Purchase?
faq.answer: CSIR will prepare a single merit list. Cadre allocation (General / Finance & Accounts / Stores & Purchase) will be decided based on merit and the candidate's preferred cadre choice submitted in the online application form. Once allotted, the cadre is final and cannot be changed.

faq.question: Q10. What documents are required at the time of CSIR ASO document verification?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry original documents including proof of date of birth, educational qualification certificates (10th onwards), CSE-2024 mark sheets (Prelims and Mains + Personality Test), caste/community certificate (if applicable), PwBD certificate (if applicable), employment certificate, and a No Objection Certificate from employer (if currently in government service). Self-attested copies of all uploaded documents must also be brought.
